codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the new standardized DTOs, facade methods, migration-backed journal typing/source metadata, and manual/list/reverse endpoints. However, the requirement that all cross-module journal creation flows route through the standardized AccountingService facade path is not fully met: multiple factory/production flows still call non-standard facade methods that build raw JournalEntryRequest and invoke accountingService.createJournalEntry directly instead of createStandardJournal(JournalCreationRequest).

issues.description: postMaterialConsumption still constructs JournalEntryRequest and calls accountingService.createJournalEntry directly (line 704) instead of routing through createStandardJournal(JournalCreationRequest). This violates the explicit requirement to standardize all cross-module journal creation through the facade standard DTO path.

postLaborOverheadApplied also bypasses the standardized flow by building JournalEntryRequest and calling accountingService.createJournalEntry directly (line 803), leaving a cross-module factory/production posting path unstandardized.

postSimpleJournal (used by factory modules) posts via accountingService.createJournalEntry (line 1515) and does not set standardized sourceModule/sourceReference/journalType metadata via JournalCreationRequest. Because ProductionLogService and PackingService still use this API, not all cross-module operations are standardized as requested.

Production flow still calls accountingFacade.postSimpleJournal for semi-finished receipt posting. That method bypasses createStandardJournal, so this cross-module journal creation flow remains outside the standardized contract.

Packing flow continues to call accountingFacade.postSimpleJournal (verified in commit grep), which bypasses createStandardJournal. This contradicts the requirement that journal creation be cleanly standardized through JournalCreationRequest + createStandardJournal.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The transcript skeleton in this mission stores only a compact summary string, so there is no step-level tool trace or skillFeedback for validating whether backend-worker procedure (especially TDD-first) was actually followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/realnigga/.factory/missions/9cf481ca-5e0f-423d-8241-8364a13a7137/worker-transcripts.jsonl contains one record for this session with keys {workerSessionId, featureId, milestone, skeleton, timestamp}; extracted metadata shows skillFeedback=null and no per-step event records.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance and backend-worker skill still prescribe event-driven cross-module communication, but this feature requirement explicitly forbids introducing ApplicationEvents/listeners for journal creation and expects direct standardized facade calls; the guidance is outdated/contradictory for accounting journal flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line 39: 'Event-driven: Cross-module side effects should use Spring ApplicationEvents, not direct service calls'; feature description requires 'DO NOT introduce Spring ApplicationEvents or event listeners for journal creation. Keep existing direct call pattern'. backend-worker/SKILL.md line 41 repeats event-driven guidance.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit de122309, diff, and transcript skeleton. Core standardized journal/manual APIs were added, but the feature fails acceptance because factory/production posting paths still bypass createStandardJournal via legacy AccountingFacade methods (postMaterialConsumption, postLaborOverheadApplied, postSimpleJournal) used by ProductionLogService/PackingService.
